Jon Vaught for and with Marquez Simmons, Personal Representative (via Zoom).

Mr. Disston reported that a deed of trust in favor of the City and County of San Francisco, related to a bail bond posted for Mark Simmons in 2013, remains unresolved. The bail bond was exonerated in 2014, but no order for reconveyance was issued. A certified copy of the criminal case's minute order is needed to address the reconveyance [SF Criminal Case No. 10036845]. Obtaining this document is expected to take up to 30 days.

Discussion was held regarding a settlement agreement requiring Mark Simmons' signature. Discrepancies were noted regarding the source and purpose of funds for resolving issues with Rocket Mortgage and covering move-out costs. Mr. Vaught reported that the Order Expunging Lis Pendens obtained last week has yet to be recorded.

The court continued the matter to January 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, for further updates. Counsel were instructed to meet and confer to resolve issues with the settlement agreement. Mr. Disston was also instructed to email a copy of the deed of trust to all parties and to the probate examiner.

The court noted the pending trustee sale scheduled for January 8, 2025, and inquired about efforts to prevent it.

Mr. Vaught reported arranging a private lender willing to provide funds to cover the outstanding mortgage and back taxes totaling approximately $60,000. The court acknowledged the urgency of resolving the lis pendens and preventing the trustee sale and suggested that a drop-off ex parte application could be prepared to resolve the lis pendens quickly.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marquez Simmons (executor) appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Timothy Batts appeared by zoom for Mark Simmons (heir).
Steven Schroeder appeared by zoom for Inheritance Funding Company Inc.

Mr. Batts stated that they gathered documents from Rocket Mortgage, that there was a proposal to purchase his client's interest in the estate, but the time to accept it expired because his client was involved in an accident and was hospitalized, and that if given additional time, he feels they could get a deal worked out. Mr. Simmons stated that his plan is to list the property for sale and close this out, that it has been nine years and that he is not interested in waiting any longer. The Court responded that that does not mean that it can grant an 850 petition to put this into the trust and that if there is no personal representative you cannot notice that person. PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-29-2015 Denied

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-29-2015 Continued To Jan-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Taylor for (petitioner) Marquez Simmons. Terry Szucsko appearing on behalf of Mila Lvovich for and with (objector) Mark Simmons. David Simmons in Propria Persona. Elaine Gratneyeses (objector) in Propria Persona.

The Court asked David Simmons if he anything further to report. Mr. Simmons stated that there is a lot of discrepancies regarding who wants to be trustee. The Court stated that we are not here on the trust matter, that this is for probate. Mr. Simmons indicated that he did not understand and the court went over the documents that have been filed. Mr. Simmons stated that he is against the others and that the estate is worth more than $75,000.00. Mr. Taylor stated that he has nothing in response and confirmed that the estate is worth more than $75,000.00 and that there is a house in San Francisco. Ms. Gratneyeses stated that there is property in Vallejo that should be in the estate. Mr. Taylor reported that there is a Quiet Title action in Solano County dealing with that property, that prior to the death of Ms. Simmons the property was given to Marquez Simmons and that it is being contested that the transfer was not proper. Mr. Szucsko stated that Mark Simmons was just noticed of these proceedings and requested a 30 day continuance to file a petition/objections and straighten things out. Mr. Taylor stated that he has no objection to the continuance, that there is an issue regarding the lost codicil and that they do not think the appearance of Mark Simmons attorney will straighten things out. The Court stated that it will deny the Petition for Letters of Administration filed by David Simmons. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to January 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Gratneyeses stated that there are additional properties in Arizona, Arkansas and California. David Simmons stated that they keep going over this and pro-longing the proceedings, that they were in Arizona and knew about the properties but hid it from the Court, that Mark Simmons knew about the Arizona proceedings and that everything is being dragged out. Mr. Taylor confirmed the proceedings in Arizona. The Court stated that it can only deal with this estate, not what is going on in Arizona. The Court asked about Earl Dear and was told by Mr. Simmons that he is deceased. Mr. Taylor was instructed to give new notice and to publish.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy C. Robles. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Simmons; Joyce Simmons; Evan Cote (by CourtCall); Heather Cote (by CourtCall).

Mr. Cote presented arguments for the granting of the motion to be relieved as counsel. Mr. Simmons responded to the arguments of Mr. Cote. Ms. Cote added further grounds for granting of the motion. Regarding the hearing on Mr. Simmons' pending petition for letters of administration set for hearing on December 29, 2015, should the motion to withdraw be granted the Court asked Mr. Simmons his preference between the petition hearing being continued or going forward as scheduled. Mr. Simmons stated that his preference was for the hearing to go forward as scheduled on December 29th. The Court granted the motion to be relieved as counsel (order signed in open court), effective immediately.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported).
